---
title: "Margin Table with R gt"
papersize: us-letter
format:
  typst:
    keep-typ: true
_quarto:
  tests:
    typst:
      ensureTypstFileRegexMatches:
        # R gt in margin divs: #note() contains div content (warnings),
        # but #figure() is OUTSIDE the note (figure NOT in margin - broken!)
        # Different from Python gt which wraps figure inside note
        - ['#note\(', '#figure\(', 'kind: "quarto-float-tbl"']
        - []
      ensurePdfRegexMatches:
        - ['Table 1', 'Large Landmasses', 'Asia', 'Africa']
        - []
      noErrors: default
---

This tests an R gt table placed in the margin using a fenced div.

::: {.column-margin}

```{r}
#| classes: plain
#| echo: false
#| label: tbl-islands-r
#| tbl-cap: "Large Landmasses"

library(gt)
library(dplyr)

islands_tbl <- tibble(
  name = names(islands),
  size = islands
) |>
  arrange(desc(size)) |>
  slice(1:10)

gt(islands_tbl) |>
  fmt_integer(columns = size)
```

:::

See @tbl-islands-r for the table in the margin.

More text in the main column to show the layout.
